sections.heading: What is an EMI?
sections.body: Equated Monthly Installment (EMI) is a fixed payment amount made by a borrower to a lender at a specified date each calendar month. EMIs are used to pay off both interest and principal each month so that over a specified number of years, the loan is fully cleared.

sections.heading: The EMI Formula
sections.body: EMI = [P x R x (1+R)^N]/[((1+R)^N)-1], where P is the Principal loan amount, R is the monthly interest rate, and N is the number of monthly installments. Each month, the interest is calculated on the outstanding balance, and the remaining EMI amount goes towards reducing the principal.

sections.heading: Reducing Balance vs Flat Rate
sections.body: In reducing balance (standard), interest is calculated on the remaining principal. In flat rate, interest is calculated on the total loan amount throughout the tenure, which is much more expensive. Always choose reducing balance loans!
